title: Chrysanthemum lavandulifolium
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum lavandulifolium is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a type of chrysanthemum, a genus of plants that is native to Asia. The genus Chrysanthemum is further divided into several sections and subsections based on their characteristics. Chrysanthemum lavandulifolium is often classified as part of the section Discedenses, which includes plants with divided leaves. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and is used in botanical and horticultural contexts.

intro: Chrysanthemum lavandulifolium, also known as the white chrysanthemum, is a perennial herb that belongs to the Asteraceae family, commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine. The health benefits of Chrysanthemum lavandulifolium include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-modulating properties, which are said to help alleviate fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, such as ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Chrysanthemum lavandulifolium is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
